⏺ To stop the background agent and resume here:

  1. Open the agents panel — run claude agents in a new terminal, or press
  Ctrl+\ inside the Claude Code session where the agent is running.
  2. Find the running agent — it will be listed with its task description.
  3. Stop it — select it and press s (stop), or run claude agents stop 
  <task-id>.
  4. Resume here — once stopped, run /resume again in this session and it should
  load the prior context.

  Alternatively, if you don't need that session's state, you can just start
  fresh here — /resume without a prior stopped session will just begin a new
  conversation.

⏺ Deep research is running in the background — it'll fan out 5 parallel search

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The workflow runs 5 independent search agents simultaneously (masonry CSS,
  bento grids, card density UX, mod browser patterns, information hierarchy),
  then adversarially verifies every factual claim with a 3-vote panel before it
  makes it into the final report. This eliminates the "confident but wrong"
  results you get from a single search.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  While it runs — the three browser variations in Upgraded/ use a uniform
  3-column CSS grid right now. The research will tell us whether native CSS
  masonry, a JS-assisted approach, or a bento-style layout would work best for
  cards with variable description lengths. The answer will directly feed into
  the next generate.py revision.

  Spotlight Border Card, Drag-to-Pan Grid.
